titleStmt.title: SII 40.275: original edition by K. Munirathnam
No. 275. (A.R. No. B. 320 of 1933.) Nārṇepāḍu, Sattenapalle Taluk, Guntur District, Andhra Pradesh.

summary.p: On a broken Nandi pillar opposite to the temple of Bhīmēśvarasvāmi.
This is dated Śaka 1073, Uttarāyaṇa Saṅkrānti.

Records the gift of 55 sheep for burning a perpetual lamp to the god Sōmēśvaradēva of Nārāyaṇapāḍu by Prōlanāmātya of Bhāradvāja gōtra, who styles himself the lord of Origāṇḍipura, for the merit of his parents. The gift was entrusted to Kanḍiyabolliyabōya, for supply of ghee to the temple.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti śrī śakavarṣamulu 1073 gunēṁṭi yuttarāyaṇa saṁkrānti
nimittamuna nāpastaṁbakulatilakuṇḍunu bāradvāja gōtruṇḍu
gōtrapavitruṇḍunu vibudha haricandanuṇḍunu norigāṁ
varēśuṇḍununaina prōlanāmātyuṇḍu damatallidaṁḍrulaku
dharmmamugā śrīnārāyaṇapāṭi śrī sōmēśvara mahādēva
rakunakhaṇḍavartti dīpamunakuṁ beṭṭina goṟiyalu 55 vīni
jēkoni komanabōyu koḍku kaṇḍiya bolliya bōyuṇḍu danapu
pautrikamācaṁdrārkkamu nityamāneṇḍu nēyi dī
paṁbunakuṁ bōyaṁggalavāṇḍu
Digital edition of SII 40.275 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
